Assignments.ParameterValue: Thanks all. This ad-hoc is our dress rehearsal before we move Corso into next week's executive sync. Yesterday gave us the raw pieces; today we turn them into a proposal Marta Bellini can take seriously.
Corso Pilot Architecture - Six Stores, Middleware Adapter, Residency Controls

The architecture story is clean if we stay disciplined. Six stores connect through the middleware adapter, data remains in-region for all operational reporting, and the merchandising hub only gets the nightly summary feed. That lets us promise speed without pretending we solved every legacy dependency.

That works well with the commercial narrative. Marta told me this morning that she wants something her finance director and operations head can both say yes to. So the proposal should read as a controlled pilot with expansion logic, not a giant multi-country transformation on day one.

The numbers support that. A 180-thousand pilot across six flagship stores funds the residency controls, training, and governance. If success metrics are hit by day ninety, the commercial path expands into the 1.8 million ARR network agreement with no need to renegotiate the full pricing model.

The Nordic Apparel reference call went very well yesterday. Their COO explicitly said our rollout discipline and training model were two reasons they moved quickly. I have permission to quote that in the partner-review appendix, which should calm the finance side at Corso.

Excellent. I will also take the executive-sponsor ask myself for now and tell Marta that Mat can join once we reach proposal stage. That keeps the story senior enough without overplaying our hand before the customer is ready.

Then the final structure is clear: six-store pilot, ninety-day proof window, Nordic reference for operational credibility, and a named executive path if the customer wants chain-wide expansion. I can finalize the narrative tonight and be ready to ask for a formal week-two proposal on Tuesday.
Pilot economics appendix is final: 180K pilot, success metrics by day 90, conversion path to 1.8M ARR partnership.
Nordic reference quote approved for use in the Corso appendix.

Great work. Facilitator, summarize the Milan partner review with the six-store pilot scope, the 180K commercial anchor, and the executive-sponsor escalation path so we can feed all of it into next Tuesday's E2E sync.
Summarize the Corso partner-review outcome and prepare a concise input for the week-two EMEA proposal meeting.
